title: Unit-fraction exponents
description: Evaluate numerical expressions with unit-fraction exponents or radicals, and convert between the two forms of representations.

assessment_items.question_content:
**Which radical expression is equivalent to $x^{^{\scriptsize\dfrac18}}$?**

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints:
This is the definition for a unit-fraction exponent:

$x^{^{\scriptsize\dfrac1n}}=\sqrt[n]{x}$

[[☃ explanation 1]]
$x^{^{\scriptsize\dfrac18}}=\sqrt[8]{x}$

assessment_items.question_content:
**Which radical expression is equivalent to $y^{^{\scriptsize\dfrac17}}$?**

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints:
This is the definition for a unit-fraction exponent:

$x^{^{\scriptsize\dfrac1n}}=\sqrt[n]{x}$

[[☃ explanation 1]]
$y^{^{\scriptsize\dfrac17}}=\sqrt[7]{y}$

assessment_items.question_content:
**Which radical expression is equivalent to $z^{^{\scriptsize\dfrac16}}$?**

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints:
This is the definition for a unit-fraction exponent:

$x^{^{\scriptsize\dfrac1n}}=\sqrt[n]{x}$

[[☃ explanation 1]]
$z^{^{\scriptsize\dfrac16}}=\sqrt[6]{z}$

assessment_items.question_content:
**Which radical expression is equivalent to $a^{^{\scriptsize\dfrac15}}$?**

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints:
This is the definition for a unit-fraction exponent:

$x^{^{\scriptsize\dfrac1n}}=\sqrt[n]{x}$

[[☃ explanation 1]]
$a^{^{\scriptsize\dfrac15}}=\sqrt[5]{a}$

assessment_items.question_content:
**Which radical expression is equivalent to $b^{^{\scriptsize\dfrac14}}$?**

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints:
This is the definition for a unit-fraction exponent:

$x^{^{\scriptsize\dfrac1n}}=\sqrt[n]{x}$

[[☃ explanation 1]]
$b^{^{\scriptsize\dfrac14}}=\sqrt[4]{b}$

assessment_items.question_content:
**Which radical expression is equivalent to $k^{^{\scriptsize\dfrac19}}$?**

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints:
This is the definition for a unit-fraction exponent:

$x^{^{\scriptsize\dfrac1n}}=\sqrt[n]{x}$

[[☃ explanation 1]]
$k^{^{\scriptsize\dfrac19}}=\sqrt[9]{k}$

assessment_items.question_content:
**Which radical expression is equivalent to $t^{^{\scriptsize\dfrac13}}$?**

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints:
This is the definition for a unit-fraction exponent:

$x^{^{\scriptsize\dfrac1n}}=\sqrt[n]{x}$

[[☃ explanation 1]]
$t^{^{\scriptsize\dfrac13}}=\sqrt[3]{t}$

assessment_items.question_content:
**Which radical expression is equivalent to $c^{^{\scriptsize\dfrac12}}$?**

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints:
This is the definition for a unit-fraction exponent:

$x^{^{\scriptsize\dfrac1n}}=\sqrt[n]{x}$

[[☃ explanation 1]]
$c^{^{\scriptsize\dfrac12}}=\sqrt[2]{c}=\sqrt{c}$

assessment_items.question_content:
**Which exponential expression is equivalent to $\sqrt[8]{y}$?**

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints:
This is the definition for a unit-fraction exponent:

$x^{^{\scriptsize\dfrac1n}}=\sqrt[n]{x}$

[[☃ explanation 1]]

Applying the definition in the reverse direction, we get

$\sqrt[8]{y}=y^{^{\scriptsize\dfrac18}}$.

assessment_items.question_content:
**Which exponential expression is equivalent to $\sqrt[4]{x}$?**

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints:
This is the definition for a unit-fraction exponent:

$x^{^{\scriptsize\dfrac1n}}=\sqrt[n]{x}$

[[☃ explanation 1]]

Applying the definition in the reverse direction, we get

$\sqrt[4]{x}=x^{^{\scriptsize\dfrac14}}$.

assessment_items.question_content:
**Which exponential expression is equivalent to $\sqrt[9]{z}$?**

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints:
This is the definition for a unit-fraction exponent:

$x^{^{\scriptsize\dfrac1n}}=\sqrt[n]{x}$

[[☃ explanation 1]]

Applying the definition in the reverse direction, we get

$\sqrt[9]{z}=z^{^{\scriptsize\dfrac19}}$.

assessment_items.question_content:
**Which exponential expression is equivalent to $\sqrt[3]{a}$?**

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints:
This is the definition for a unit-fraction exponent:

$x^{^{\scriptsize\dfrac1n}}=\sqrt[n]{x}$

[[☃ explanation 1]]

Applying the definition in the reverse direction, we get

$\sqrt[3]{a}=a^{^{\scriptsize\dfrac13}}$.

assessment_items.question_content:
**Which exponential expression is equivalent to $\sqrt[7]{b}$?**

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints:
This is the definition for a unit-fraction exponent:

$x^{^{\scriptsize\dfrac1n}}=\sqrt[n]{x}$

[[☃ explanation 1]]

Applying the definition in the reverse direction, we get

$\sqrt[7]{b}=b^{^{\scriptsize\dfrac17}}$.

assessment_items.question_content:
**Which exponential expression is equivalent to $\sqrt[5]{c}$?**

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints:
This is the definition for a unit-fraction exponent:

$x^{^{\scriptsize\dfrac1n}}=\sqrt[n]{x}$

[[☃ explanation 1]]

Applying the definition in the reverse direction, we get

$\sqrt[5]{c}=c^{^{\scriptsize\dfrac15}}$.

assessment_items.question_content:
**Which exponential expression is equivalent to $\sqrt{k}$?**

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints:
This is the definition for a unit-fraction exponent:

$x^{^{\scriptsize\dfrac1n}}=\sqrt[n]{x}$

[[☃ explanation 1]]

Applying the definition in the reverse direction, we get

$\sqrt{k}=\sqrt[2]{k}=k^{^{\scriptsize\dfrac12}}$.

assessment_items.question_content:
**Which exponential expression is equivalent to $\sqrt[6]{t}$?**

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints:
This is the definition for a unit-fraction exponent:

$x^{^{\scriptsize\dfrac1n}}=\sqrt[n]{x}$

[[☃ explanation 1]]

Applying the definition in the reverse direction, we get

$\sqrt[6]{t}=t^{^{\scriptsize\dfrac16}}$.
